records.ety: [F., fr. L. ruralis, fr. rus, ruris, the country. Cf. Room space, Rustic.]
usage: Syn. -- Rustic. -- Rural, Rustic. Rural refers to the country itself; as, rural scenes, prospects, delights, etc. Rustic refers to the character, condition, taste, etc., of the original inhabitants of the country, who were generally uncultivated and rude; as, rustic manners; a rustic dress; a rustic bridge; rustic architecture, etc. [1913 Webster] We turn To where the silver Thames first rural grows. Thomson. [1913 Webster] Lay bashfulness, that rustic virtue, by; To manly confidence thy throughts apply. Dryden. [1913 Webster]

defs: Of or pertaining to the country, as distinguished from a city or town; living in the country; suitable for, or resembling, the country; rustic; as, rural scenes; a rural prospect.
quotes: Here is a rural fellow; . . . He brings you figs.

defs: Of or pertaining to agriculture; as, rural economy.
quotes: We turn To where the silver Thames first rural grows.
Lay bashfulness, that rustic virtue, by; To manly confidence thy throughts apply.
